findings.description: Stored (second-order) XSS: event['display_name'] from the users table is interpolated raw into HTML <td> with no html.escape(), so an attacker who registers an account with a script payload as their display name executes JavaScript in the admin's authenticated session when the activity report is viewed (CWE-79, OWASP A05 Injection).

Reflected/stored XSS: the caller-supplied 'title' is inserted unescaped via PAGE_TEMPLATE.format into both <title> and <h1>, allowing arbitrary script injection if title derives from any user-influenced source (CWE-79, OWASP A05).

Stored XSS via event['action']: the action field (commonly derived from user-driven request/endpoint data) is written into the HTML table with no escaping, providing a second untrusted injection vector into the admin dashboard (CWE-79, OWASP A05).

No defense-in-depth against XSS: the emitted full HTML document sets no Content-Security-Policy (no meta CSP, no header control), so the unescaped-output XSS above has no mitigating layer (CWE-1021/CWE-79, OWASP A02 Security Misconfiguration: missing security headers).

Potential path traversal / arbitrary file write: save_report passes caller-controlled out_path straight to open(out_path,'w') with no base-directory confinement or '..'/absolute-path validation, enabling overwrite of arbitrary files if out_path is ever reachable from external input (CWE-22/CWE-73, OWASP A01).

event['created_at'] is concatenated into HTML unescaped; low risk because it is a DB-generated timestamp, but it is still an unescaped sink that becomes exploitable if the column ever stores attacker-controlled text (CWE-79).
Log forging/injection: 'title' (untrusted) is logged without stripping newlines/control chars, so a crafted title can inject forged lines into the reports log (CWE-117, OWASP A09).

Format-string vector considered and dismissed: PAGE_TEMPLATE is a trusted constant and untrusted values are passed as .format() value-arguments (not the format spec), and str.format does not recursively expand braces in substituted values, so no CWE-134-style injection here — reported for completeness only.
Off-by-one vs. contract (non-security, deferred to logic critic): docstring says 'both boundary days included' but the query uses created_at < ? making 'end' exclusive, so the last day is dropped.

top_users 'limit' is parameterized as ? (safe from SQLi) but is unvalidated; a non-int or huge value from a caller could raise or cause an unbounded result set / memory pressure rather than any injection (CWE-20, mostly DoS-adjacent, low concern in current scope).
